dress-code-policy-students-and-staff/sta:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

Staff members are expected to dress in business casual attire, avoiding overly casual or inappropriate clothing in the professional setting. This includes avoiding items such as jeans, flip flops, and tank tops during school hours.
dress-code-policy-students-and-staff/stu:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

Students are required to wear school uniforms consisting of a white shirt, navy blue trousers or skirts, and black shoes. The uniform should always be neat and tidy, reflecting our commitment to maintaining high standards of appearance.
dress-code-policy-students-and-staff/acc:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

The use of excessive or distracting accessories, such as large jewelry or brightly colored headbands, is discouraged in order to maintain focus within the educational setting.
dress-code-policy-students-and-staff/enf:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

Non-compliance with the dress code may result in warnings, parental notification, or other disciplinary actions. Our school believes in corrective measures that emphasize learning and growth over punishment.
dress-code-policy-students-and-staff/pri:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

At Imagine Montessori School Valencia, we believe in fostering a respectful and professional environment. Our dress code reflects the values of discipline, self-respect, and equality, aiming to create a positive atmosphere conducive to learning and personal growth.
staff-dress-code/acceptable-clothing/sta:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

**Acceptable clothing** includes: appropriate-length skirts and dresses (knee-length), blouses, smart t-shirts, polo shirts, trousers, smart jeans, chinos.
staff-dress-code/unacceptable-clothing/s:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

**Not acceptable:**

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts shorter than mid-thigh.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear (except for PE and associated activities).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts; low-cut tops; crop-tops; vest/spaghetti-strap tops.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Clothing with tears, holes, or rips; clothes bearing offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Indoor wearing of baseball caps or hats; high-heeled stiletto shoes or beach flip-flops (indoors).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment (loose pockets, sequins, large buttons).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Underwear worn in a revealing manner.
student-dress-code-general-guidance/foot:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Flat, enclosed shoes must be worn outdoors — comfortable, fastened with laces, buckles, zips, or velcro, and students must be able to put them on and take them off independently.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Indoor shoes must be closed-toe to ensure safe walking; no bulky, furry, or flip-flop styles. Anti-slip socks are also recommended.
student-dress-code-general-guidance/head:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Hats will only be worn during playground time to protect from the sun.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Religious headwear may always be worn, provided it does not obscure the student's vision; it must be removed for all PE lessons for health and safety reasons.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Hair can be any length and any style. For PE, long hair must be tied back for safety.
student-dress-code-general-guidance/pe-d:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Students must wear appropriate sports underwear, shorts/leggings/tracksuit bottoms, a t-shirt or sweatshirt, and trainers.
student-dress-code-general-guidance/prin:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

As an inclusive school, we ask that our whole community respects these guidelines.
student-dress-code-general-guidance/styl:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Jewellery must not dangle; no hoop earrings or large necklaces (health and safety). On PE days, students must be able to remove stud earrings or other items as required.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Subtle make-up may be worn from Year 7 upwards (e.g., mascara, powder, foundation, nail varnish); no lipstick.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Acrylic nails may not be longer than two millimetres from the fingertip (health and safety).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Shorts and skirts must cover the upper thighs (¾ length or longer). Cycling shorts are permitted on PE days but must also cover the upper thighs.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

All clothes must be without holes or rips. All underwear must be covered by outerwear.
